item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Sanford: … A fishing hook?
D-2468-6: Yeah. Those crooked metal things you put on your fishing rod. Only a hell lot bigger and lodged in my cheek.
Dr. Sanford: We didn't find any wounds on your person.

D-3468-6: I-I know, but it was there, Lord knows it was.

Dr. Sanford: I believe you. Continue. What happened when you fell into the water?

Is it a spelling incorrect or on purpose?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahem, it has been 3 years and I still see those mistakes he mentioned…

Worth the necro-post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok so the person almost got eaten, and then he's (or is it a she?) made into a D-class.
Damn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it. The moment when you realize the child isn't the lanternfish- it's something else's lantern- is a great one.

For people concerned with the nature of the fishermen (why do they look like fish, why do they speak English, etc)- consider the fact that the returning D-class didn't actually have a hook in their cheek, nor any sign that a hook had been there. My interpretation is that their memory of the experience on the other end of the "fishing line" isn't literally what happened to them, it's a symbolic representation assembled by their brain trying to make sense of whatever they actually experienced. There wasn't any hook, there weren't any fish-with-legs, there wasn't any English (there may not have been any speech)- there were things that they came to understand as fitting those symbolic roles, and their brain filled in the rest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A school of manerfish, even.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would this be a manerfish?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Technically speaking, wouldn't it be Fish peopleing for people?

*dun dun tss*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the wounded-child-as-bait bit, but "Fish fishing for people" is way too corny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, this thing is WAY too similar to SCP-957 for me to be okay with (though, because of it being so similar, it could make a good counter keter). I'll let it slide.

Not to mention, I would have preferred it if you made the "bait" something like a sandwich or a turkey or something, since fish snag on a line because there's food on it, not because there's a hurt baby fish on it.

reluctant upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the ghost girl lure. I hate the giant fish-ermen. This should either have something weirder and less campy as the payoff or be a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh… it's well-written and I kind of like it, and the fishhook was initially scary as hell, and then it went in a purely goofy direction. Evens out to a no vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was honestly hoping for an angler-fish-type-creature with a fake human child for a lure poking its lure into our world from Somewhere Else™. As it is, it comes across a fair bit trite with a helping of "nudge-nudge-wink-wink, get it, it's a joke, see?" More something I'd expect to see in a 1930-40's cartoon (where it was done to death, resurrected, done to death again, then had its corpse beaten with the corpses of half a dozen other overdone old cartoon tropes).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…fish catching people.

Take your upvote and get out

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like Keter might be too high for this. It's only killed 16 people since the 1800s, if I'm reading this right. Despite being not entirely contained, I think Euclid would be a better fit. Still, I like it enough to +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but there's an awful lot of awkward phrasing and redundant statements. I can't itemize it because I'm on my phone, but there's quite a bit of it in there. No-vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing with DrBerggren. I actually was creeped out by the ending, knowing that not everyone gets thrown back. Sounded like they were looking for a "fish" to slap on the grill and serve to their family.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What exactly do you mean by, it would make a pretty good -J? -Js are supposed to be funny. This is just a damn well done twist ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find myself thinking of this one as a pretty good -J, but not so much a mainlister.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made me laugh. I wanna make contact with the entities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it but it has already been done before with a spider clouds SCP….

+1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one! Good work, Doc Sandford, I look forward to seeing more from you in the future. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tone seems a bit un-clinical to me for most of this article, but I like the idea. Declined to rate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't talking about the article, I was talking about fishermen who make a living off of fishing, which I assume he was talking about.

As for the article, I really like the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, but please note the part where there's three of them and one of them is larger than both.

This isn't some fishermen mass-fishing for money.

This is a fishing trip. With a father, and two children.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I bet humans are like the highest quality to these fishmen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, there are some small fish that are in high demand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because fishermen don't keep catches that are too small.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well it got my upvote. But I'm not sure about why the SCP targets things with above 55 Kilograms. Is there some sort of significance to it? Though this might be my problem since it's about midnight where I am and I skimmed a few parts. :|

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your complaints have been registered and will soon be taken into effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure this is an exemplary skip or anything, but human fishing is worth an upvote where I'm from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In all honesty, I found the punchline rather trite and I second the inexplicable english comment by Thekillerax (the same thing that bugged me about the "To Serve Man" twilight zone episode - that a double-entendre like that inexplicably worked in an alien language, nvm how the hell did they translate it).

Also, couple minor problems.

SCP-2468 tends to appear in populated yet open areas, including urban parks and town squares. The subject has shown a tendency to revisit places which have resulted in a successful yield.

Why is it referred to suddenly as subject after being called entity for two paragraphs before? Makes it sound as if you're talking about something else.

Upon reaching a distance of thirty-five centimeters from SCP-2468, victims are lifted by an unknown force to a height of approximately two meters, and dematerialize completely, along with SCP-2468.

This sentence is confusing, straight after describing calls for help, it suddenly talks about victims. I suggest this replacement.

If a living organism of sufficient size ([describe limits, author]) approaches within 35cm of SCP-2468, it is lifted by an unknown force to a height of approximately two metres, then dematerializes, along with SCP-2468.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am going to refrain from making an obvious Soviet Russia joke and instead question why the big fish was speaking in English for the "no meat" sentence. I mean, even for someone who doesn't participate in fishing, it'd still be pretty obvious as to why he was rejected. I'm also a bit iffy on making the human fishers actual fish… It reads like something out of a comic book or a really bad joke book.
Issues aside, I really liked this and will give it a upvote, but I kinda wish it was a bit more subtle and less Soviet-Russia-y.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, I remember this one! I wrote the first lines of the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is alright. I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed them. Sorry. Thought I took care of them all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Added rating module.

You have some XXXX's.
